design_philosophy.core_loop: Physical interaction with R.O.B. robot peripheral to manipulate on-screen blocks and solve spatial puzzles
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Synchronization between player input, on-screen game state, and R.O.B.'s slow physical response time; managing R.O.B.'s limited visual recognition
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Operating a sophisticated robotic companion as an interactive partner; experiencing video games as a hybrid physical-digital toy experience rather than pure software
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Stack-Up commits entirely to R.O.B. peripheral integration as its core identity. It rejects traditional controller-only gameplay to create a unique 'sophisticated toy experience' that positions gaming as hardware engineering. The game exists primarily to market the robot accessory rather than as a standalone software experience; it's deliberately designed around hardware limitations (R.O.B.'s slow movement, optical recognition constraints) as intentional design features rather than obstacles to overcome.

difficulty.lives_system: Multiple lives with game-over condition; resets on failure
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based progression; failure returns player to stage start
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player must manually coordinate timing with R.O.B.'s slow autonomous movement; limited direct control creates asymmetrical puzzle-solving where player plans moves ahead for robot execution. Success depends on environmental setup and timing prediction rather than reflexes.

progression.unlock_mechanism: Stage-based progression; complete objectives to advance to next stage
replayability_hooks: Challenge in optimizing synchronization with R.O.B.'s movement patterns

danger_signals: R.O.B.'s optical recognition dependency creates failure states invisible to player
Slow robot response time can frustrate players expecting real-time gameplay feedback

sources_used: tcrf - Stack-Up The Cutting Room Floor (developer credits, release dates, R.O.B. series context)
wikipedia - R.O.B. (marketing strategy, hardware design intent, gameplay philosophy, developer commentary on Gyromite mechanics)
